summary.intent: Cut release tag 20260327.01 for moltis and fix post-release bugs reported in issue #493 related to install script version parsing and package naming.
summary.outcome: Release 20260327.01 was successfully published after manually recovering cancelled Docker arm64 build jobs. Two bugs were fixed: install.sh version parsing/URL prefix logic for non-v-prefixed tags, and cargo deb/rpm packaging using wrong version from Cargo.toml instead of the release tag.

repo: Release tags use date-based format (YYYYMMDD.NN, e.g. 20260327.01) without a 'v' prefix, unlike older semver tags (v0.10.18).
cargo deb and cargo generate-rpm both read version from Cargo.toml (0.1.0) by default — release.yml must pass --deb-version and --set-metadata flags to override with the actual release version.
Homebrew and Arch package builds already used the tag-derived VERSION correctly; only deb and rpm builds had the version mismatch bug.
website/releases.json is updated as part of the release workflow to trigger auto-updates for users on the current stable version.

code.finding: get_latest_version() used sed regex 's/.*"v([^"]+)".*/\1/' which requires a 'v' prefix in the tag — fails silently for date-based tags, returning raw JSON instead of the version string.
All download URLs used 'v${version}' prefix. Fixed by adding a release_tag() helper that conditionally applies the 'v' prefix based on tag format.
cargo deb invocation lacked --deb-version flag; cargo generate-rpm lacked --set-metadata version override. Both caused packages to ship with Cargo.toml version (0.1.0) instead of release tag.

workflow: GitHub Actions re-run only applies to explicitly selected jobs — re-running amd64 Docker build does not automatically re-trigger the cancelled arm64 job.
Re-running a job while another job in the same run is still in progress is blocked by the API; must wait for dependent jobs to complete first.
Background task polling was used to wait for amd64 completion before triggering arm64 re-run.

friction: arm64 Docker build was cancelled mid-release for an unclear reason (timeout or runner issue), requiring manual intervention.
GitHub API blocked re-run of arm64 job while amd64 was still running, requiring polling and delayed retry.
Version format change (semver with 'v' prefix → date-based without prefix) silently broke install.sh parsing with no immediate test coverage to catch it.
The deb/rpm version mismatch was only discovered post-release via a user-filed bug report.
open_items: No automated test or CI check validates that install.sh correctly parses the current release tag format — a format change can silently break it again.
The Cargo.toml version (0.1.0) is permanently out of sync with release tags — consider whether Cargo.toml version should track releases or remain a placeholder.
Root cause of arm64 Docker job cancellation was not identified — could recur in future releases.
